message.reasoning_content:
We need to produce JSON for stage web-003-chain-link. Stage goal: Create webpage 3 and link it back to page 2 while preserving existing page 1 and page 2 links. Expected artifact: site/page-3.html (html). Validators: page-3-links-page-2: failure_class=link_integrity; previous-links-preserved: failure_class=link_integrity. We have existing files: site/index.html and site/page-2.html. We need to create site/page-3.html that contains a link to page-2.html. Also must preserve existing links: page-2 links back to index.html. That is already present. We must not break that. So page-3 should not modify page-2. Just create new file. Also need to preserve page-1 and page-2 links. That means page-3 should not remove or alter page-2's link. But we are not editing page-2. So fine. Also need to ensure page-3 links to page-2. So include Page 2 or similar. Also need to ensure syntax: valid HTML. Also need to preserve page-1 and page-2 links: page-1 has no links. page-2 has link to index.html. That remains. Thus create site/page-3.html with minimal structure: DOCTYPE, html, head, meta, title, body, header maybe, nav maybe, main with content and link to page-2.html. Also need to ensure link integrity: page-3-links-page-2 validator will check that page-3 contains a link to page-2. So we need to include Page 2. Also previous-links-preserved validator will check that page-2 still links to index.html. Since we are not editing page-2, it's fine. Thus produce JSON with files array containing site/page-3.html. Provide content string with newlines escaped. We need to ensure content string is correct. Use double quotes inside string escaped as \". Use \n for newlines. Let's craft content: Page 3

Page 3

This is page 3 content.

Back to Page 2

Now escape: "\\n\\n\\n\\nPage 3\\n\\n\\n
\\n

Page 3

\\n
\\n
\\n
\\n
\\n

This is page 3 content.

\\n

Back to Page 2

\\n
\\n\\n" Check: we need to escape quotes around page-2.html: href=\\\"page-2.html\\\". Good. Also ensure no stray backslashes. The string uses double backslashes for JSON? Actually in JSON string, we need to escape backslashes as \\. But we are writing the content string inside JSON. We need to escape backslashes. For example, to represent \n we write \\n. To represent \" we write \\\". So the content string should be: "\\n\\n\\n\\nPage 3\\n\\n\\n
\\n

Page 3

\\n
\\n
\\n
\\n
\\n

This is page 3 content.

\\n

Back to Page 2

\\n
\\n\\n" Yes. Now produce JSON object: { "files": [ { "path": "site/page-3.html", "content": "\\n\\n\\n\\nPage 3\\n\\n\\n
\\n

Page 3

\\n
\\n
\\n
\\n
\\n

This is page 3 content.

\\n

Back to Page 2

\\n
\\n\\n" } ], "notes": "Created page-3.html linking back to page-2.html." } Check that we didn't include any markdown fences. Good. Return only JSON.
